fact: July 26 is exactly 207 days into the year, which means there are only 158 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is peak summer heat season - the sun blazes down for about 15 hours a day in many places!
Cicadas are screaming their loudest around this time of year, making that crazy buzzing sound that can reach 120 decibels - louder than a chainsaw!
July 26 falls smack in the middle of Leo season, when the zodiac lion is roaring with fiery energy and dramatic flair.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere countries, this date marks deep winter - penguins are huddling together while kids up north are swimming!
Sunflowers are typically at their most gigantic around this time in North America, with some monster blooms growing taller than basketball players!
The moon phase on July 26 changes every year, but when it's a new moon on this date, you get the darkest summer nights perfect for seeing shooting stars.
In northern places like Alaska, the sun barely sets around this time - it just dips toward the horizon and bounces back up like a yo-yo!
This date sits right in the middle of monsoon season in India, when the sky dumps buckets of rain and frogs come out to party in the puddles.
July 26 is roughly 35 days after the summer solstice, which means daylight is already starting to shrink by about 2 minutes per day in most places.
Desert lizards are often doing their crazy hot-sand dance around this time, lifting their feet up and down because the ground is literally too hot to stand on!
In many forests, this is when tree sap gets so thick and sticky from the heat that it traps bugs like natural glue - gross but cool!
